mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 7.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumer spending, directly lifting corporate earnings and driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based aggregate demand expansion, which historically precedes upward earnings revisions and sustains positive equity momentum over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint momentum of HOUST and PAYEMS fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding the 95.0 index threshold indicates high capacity utilization and supply-side efficiency gains, which compress input costs and expand profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns following INDPRO breaches above 95.0 do not statistically differ from baseline returns (bootstrap p-value > 0.10 over 3 years).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes directly reflect accelerating consumer expenditure, which constitutes the primary driver of US GDP growth and corporate revenue, producing immediate positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day Spearman correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and SPX 5-day forward returns remains below 0.05 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels surpassing 235.0 signal persistent inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy tightening, raising real discount rates and compressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL breaches 235.0, invalidating the inflation-valuation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth exceeding 200,000 concurrent with a unemployment rate drop of at least 0.2 percentage points confirms non-inflationary labor market tightening, which supports risk-on positioning and positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive information coefficient greater than 0.04 over any 3-year rolling estimation window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth rates accelerating above 2.0 percent annualized in late-cycle phases typically trigger preemptive monetary tightening and earnings deceleration, pressuring equity valuations and driving negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns following GDPC1 acceleration above 2.0% are not significantly negative (t-stat > -1.28) across a 5-year out-of-sample test.
